title: Dimeria stipiformis
taxonomy: Dimeria stipiformis belongs to the family Poaceae, commonly known as the grass family. This family includes a wide range of monocotyledonous plants. Dimeria stipiformis is further classified under the subfamily Panicoideae. The genus Dimeria itself is a group of grasses that are primarily native to Asia. It is a small genus with a limited number of species.

intro: Dimeria stipiformis, also known as Indian cup grass or thread grass, is a type of plant belonging to the Poaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its various health benefits, including the treatment of fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Bioactive constituents of Dimeria stipiformis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Dimeria stipiformis is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as decoctions and infusions, which are consumed to alleviate symptoms of various health conditions.
